strengths: 18+ years shipping production systems with 1.8 years at Dunnhumby handling millions of requests/second via microservices, async I/O, and high-performance databases—foundational backend infrastructure depth for real-time fintech systems.

Recent hands-on work at re:cinq (April–Oct 2025) on AI-agent infrastructure, MCP code generators, and agentic frameworks demonstrates current engagement with agent-system tooling and technical adaptability.

Demonstrated architectural leadership: GISCollective (event-driven, Kubernetes-deployed, open-source with active users) and multiple senior/staff roles shipping complex systems; organized engineering mentoring (refactoring dojos) showing culture-building instinct.
concerns: No demonstrated track record of building or leading engineering teams at scale; resume shows IC/senior IC progression with no evidence of scaling teams from 10–30+ people, a core CTO responsibility.

Limited multi-agent AI systems production depth: only 6 months of recent exposure at re:cinq; no evidence of architecting end-to-end agentic workflows, designing evaluation pipelines, or managing feedback loops at production scale.

No fintech or regulated-environment domain expertise; career focused on web infrastructure, direct mail automation, and open-source GIS—missing critical context for credit systems, payment flows, working capital, and compliance architecture.
response.justification: Szabo brings genuine distributed-systems depth (18+ years continuous shipping, millions req/s infrastructure, microservices) that forms a solid foundation for the backend architecture required in real-time fintech. Recent engagement with agentic frameworks (re:cinq, April–Oct 2025) shows adaptability to AI-infrastructure domains. His open-source technical leadership (GISCollective) and mentoring orientation (dojos, workshops) suggest executive-presence building blocks. The primary gap—no demonstrated team-scaling track record—is significant for a CTO needing to grow 10–30+ engineers; however, his consistent senior/staff progression, hands-on technical depth, and culture-building activities indicate readiness to grow into executive leadership with appropriate support. Fintech domain knowledge can be onboarded; distributed-systems intuition at this depth cannot be easily acquired and is the more critical hire constraint.
